steps.html: From retrofiting a decades-old robot with a Modern Brain via a Raspberry Pi 5...
...to reimagining the Control Interface via a Valve Steamdeck...

intro.summary: MARP is a robotics test-bed that bridges my FRC experience with my design skills & aesthetic!

items.NAME: 🤖 marp.brain
items.TITLE: 🧠 Raspberry Pi 5
items.MOTTO: 4-axis stepper control with dual input via USB joystick or Wi-Fi Direct UDP, low-latency H.264 video streaming & auto-stop safety
items.TEXT:
Raspberry Pi 5 Robot Controller
Drives a 4-axis robot (2 drive steppers + 2 turret steppers) using lgpio for precise GPIO timing. Written in Python (74.2%); C++ (10.0%); Shell (8.2%); HTML (5.7%); built with CMake.

Features:
  • Dual Input — Seamlessly switches between local USB joystick (Xbox) and network UDP packets (JSON on port 5005) with automatic failover
  • Wi-Fi Direct — Acts as Group Owner hotspot (DIRECT-xx-Robot-Pi5) for field connection without a router; DHCP via dnsmasq
  • Safety — Auto-stops all motors if network connection is lost for >1 second
  • Video Multiplexer — Unified MJPEG stream on port 5600 with hot-swap between sources (Pi Camera; Kinect RGB; IR; depth map); configurable resolution and quality
  • Kinect Support — Vendors libfreenect as a submodule for RGB/depth/IR; accelerometer; and motor/LED control
  • Systemd Integration — Auto-starts wifi-direct; stepper-controller; and video-stream services on boot
Hardware Pinout (BCM GPIO):
  • Left Drive: 5/6/13 · Right Drive: 19/26/21
  • Turret Pan: 23/24/25 · Turret Tilt: 12/16/20
  • Activity LED: GPIO 18
Diagrams: High-Level Wiring; Data Flow; User Story; System Architecture (Mermaid-sourced)
Dependencies: liblgpio-dev; nlohmann-json3-dev; dnsmasq; rpicam-vid; libfreenect

items.NAME: 🕹️ marp.gamepad
items.TITLE: 🎮 Valve Steamdeck
items.MOTTO: Wireless teleoperation with real-time video feed, native Steam Deck & Xbox controller support via Unity's Input System
items.TEXT:
MARP Gamepad — Wireless Teleoperation Client
A Unity 6.0 client-side control application designed primarily for the Valve Steam Deck; enabling wireless teleoperation and real-time video streaming over a local Wi-Fi network. Written in ShaderLab (44.8%); C# (43.9%); HLSL (9.2%); PowerShell (1.7%); Shell (0.4%).

Features:
  • Wireless Teleoperation — Control the robot's mobility; head movements; and expressive outputs over shared local Wi-Fi
  • Live Video Feed — Receives and displays real-time MJPEG stream from the robot's onboard cameras/sensors at http://192.168.4.1:5600/stream.mjpg
  • Input Support — Native Steam Deck controls and Xbox Elite Controller via Unity's Input System package; dual joysticks for drive (left) and turret (right)
  • Source Switching — Switch between Pi Camera; Kinect RGB; IR; and depth feeds from the client via HTTP or TCP socket
Hardware Specs:
  • Steam Deck: 5–20V USB-C PD; ≤3.0A; ≤45W; 11.7" × 4.6" × 1.9"
  • Xbox Controller: 5V; ≤1.5A; ≤7.5W (optional/dev input)
Control Protocol: UDP JSON packets to 192.168.4.1:5005 — {joysticks: {left: [x;y]; right: [x;y]}} with values from -1.0 to 1.0
Build Target: Linux (Steam Deck architecture)
Deployment: build_deploy.ps1 (PowerShell) and launch.sh for automated build and deploy
